title: Connect to Wi-Fi
summary: Get your device online by finding and joining a wireless network.
learnerPromise: Get your device online by finding and joining a wireless network.
whyItMatters: Most apps, messages, and updates need an internet connection. Knowing how to join Wi-Fi lets you get online in new places like cafés, libraries, or a friend's home.
realLifeUses: Getting online at a café or library.
Setting up internet on a new phone or laptop.
Helping a family member connect their device.
Switching to a stronger network when the current one is slow.
youWillLearn: Open the Wi-Fi settings on your device.
Select a network and enter a password.
Know what to do when a network does not connect.
miniChallenge: Connect your phone or laptop to a Wi-Fi network you have not used before.
steps: Tap the Wi-Fi section and make sure it is turned on.
Look at the list of available networks and pick the one you want.
Type the password carefully, checking for capital letters and numbers.
Wait a few seconds and look for the word 'Connected' or a checkmark.
completionCriteria: Your device shows a Wi-Fi icon and says it is connected.
You can open a web page or app that needs internet.
commonProblems: The password was typed incorrectly — try re-entering it slowly.
The network name looks similar to another one — double-check you picked the right one.
Wi-Fi is turned off — go back to settings and toggle it on.
tips: Ask the owner or staff for the correct network name and password in a new place.
If the connection fails, move a bit closer to the router or access point.
Forget old networks you no longer use so your device does not keep trying them.
